title: desmodium gyrans
intro: Desmodium gyrans, also known as Mexican firecracker, is a perennial plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant's roots and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Desmodium gyrans include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and infections. Desmodium gyrans is often prepared for medicinal use as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or salve to relieve pain and promote healing.
